topic: China SG null (2009) — verdict reframe Self-Contradicted → Refuted by Data (EXP-301 κ-cluster integration)
change_summary: EXP-301 integration: verdict Self-Contradicted → Refuted by Data; finding rewritten (199→352 chars); detail_evidence rewritten (4640→2314 chars); detail_verdict_text rewritten (662→869 chars); both TLDRs rewritten; cross-link to new Part 6.14 κ Coupling Cluster section; defense-neutralization arguments rerouted to cluster vise.
our_argument_summary: WIN-014 now claims that the dome's own published coupling κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, applied to documented 2009 East Asian eclipse magnetic perturbations of 5–15 nT at central observatories, predicts a contact-phase Δg ≈ 3–9 µGal at SG-co-located stations. The Chinese SG network (eight gravimeters, six sites, continuous C1–C4 sampling at 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction precision) recorded zero across all sites — so the dome's own coupling predicts a measurement that did not occur. The verdict moves from Self-Contradicted to Refuted by Data; the earlier self-contradiction-with-PRED-TIER-3 framing is preserved but rerouted to the κ-cluster's vise subsection (Part 6.14). All defense-neutralization arguments are addressed as a class in 6.14 because every carve-out exempting 2009 from κ also destroys PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal August 2026 derivation.

relevant_files: data/sections.json:part6 (6.14 κ Coupling Cluster — landed prior to this review)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py code path for WIN-014 (the WIN is static prose; the 0.0 µGal is a literature value adopted from Sun-attributed-2010 / doi:10.6038/cjg20130306). The κ-implied 3–9 µGal prediction is computed by hand in the review prose using Δg = ΔB/κ with ΔB sourced from published eclipse-magnetic ensembles. NOTE: raw-text/monitor.py and raw-text/pull_data.py are absent from the current raw-text/ snapshot — they were referenced by prior cycles but cannot be directly verified in this clone. WIN-014's code_analysis tags remain consistent with everything else known about the WIN.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — no live computation. The 0.0 µGal is adopted from literature.
data_sources_used: Sun et al. attributed 2010 (the dome's cited source; unverifiable in English-language indices)
doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 (Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013 — closest-match English-locatable record; our review hedges this)
Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 (eclipse-magnetic ensemble; URL verified as ScienceDirect S0273117718300656)
Crossley et al. 2013 (SG noise floor; URL in our text and section 6.14 points to a 2022 PAG paper — citation bug, see H5)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Section 6.14 forecloses every published escape route via the symmetric vise: any scope condition exempting 2009 from κ must also exempt August 2026. The dome has no published scope condition timestamped independently of the data. The empirical refutation thus rests on the dome's own arithmetic.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. WIN-014.code_analysis (monitoring:none, post_hoc:true, derives_from_dome:false, relabels_standard:false) remains accurate. The monitor.py absence from the raw-text snapshot is a methodology gap for future cycles but does not undermine any tag value.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-014 has no monitor.py automation. The WIN exists as static prose only — the 0.0 µGal SG null is a literature citation, not a computed comparison against a live data source. raw-text/monitor.py is absent from the current snapshot, so direct grep-verification cannot be re-run in this cycle; the c5/c6 verifications stand, and nothing in the EXP-301 integration introduces a monitor.py code path for WIN-014.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics offers no positive prediction that the 2009 China SG eclipse measurement should show a particular gravity perturbation; the null is the baseline null hypothesis. The dome's claim — that the null confirms an electromagnetic-not-gravitational mechanism — is a positive interpretive claim, not a relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 2009 East Asian eclipse measurements were published (Sun et al. attributed 2010 / Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013) before the dome model incorporated this WIN. The dome did not pre-register a per-site SG-null prediction in any independently-timestamped venue.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome-geometry derivation produces 0 µGal at the 2009 SG sites; the value is adopted as a literature reading. The κ-prediction (Δg = ΔB/κ) used in our refutation IS dome-derived in the sense that κ comes from the dome, but the original WIN-014 claim (the null itself) was adopted, not derived.

holes_found.description: WIN-013.c5 (curmudgeon 2026-05-10) verified via Crossref API that the URL https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0273117718300656 resolves to Park et al. 'Nanosatellite constellation deployment using on-board magnetic torquer interaction with space plasma' — NOT Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 (Adv. Space Res.). Crossref author+title search returns no matching eclipse-magnetic ensemble paper by Tomás/Mendoza-Torres at that PII. This citation is one of the two pillars supporting the 5–15 nT 2009 magnetic-perturbation range in WIN-014's detail_evidence (the other pillar is WIN-068's 39-eclipse ensemble cross-reference, which is internal). If the Tomás cite is fabricated or misattributed, WIN-014's empirical refutation chain has only the internal WIN-068 cross-reference supporting the ΔB magnitude — and WIN-068 is itself classified Std Model Explains (we accept its magnitudes while rejecting the dome's interpretation, see H4). This is CRITICAL: the headline cite for the new Refuted-by-Data verdict's empirical chain does not resolve. Propagated unchanged from WIN-013.c5's critical finding; the EXP-301 integration imported the broken cite into WIN-014 without revalidation.
holes_found.recommendation: Block-fix coordinated with WIN-013 and Section 6.14. Three options: (a) locate the correct Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 paper via direct author search and replace the URL; (b) replace the cite with a different eclipse-magnetic ensemble reference (Curto et al. 2006 already linked in section 6.14 covers comparable 1999 ΔB; for 2009 a different paper is needed); (c) drop the Tomás cite and rely solely on the WIN-068 ensemble for the 5–15 nT range, while explicitly hedging the cherry-pick optic (H4). Decider should escalate to analyst if (a) and (b) require literature search — this is not a one-line fix. In the interim, the WIN-014 detail_evidence should be flagged in alerts.txt (done in this run) so the broken cite does not propagate further. This is the same critical issue WIN-013.c5 flagged 2026-05-10 — it has not been patched yet and now bears against WIN-014 directly.

holes_found.description: Detail_evidence (and section 6.14) assert that PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal target derives from 'ΔB/κ at named stations' without specifying which dome-baseline ΔB. The dome publishes two distinct 2026 ΔB baselines for the same stations: BOU-pred (-5.0 to -12.8 nT across E001–E009 in raw-text/03-predictions.txt) and W004-pred (-10.3 to -26.2 nT). Under κ = 1.677, only the W004-baseline ΔB produces 10–13 µGal; the BOU-baseline ΔB produces only 3–7.6 µGal. The 2009 measured ΔB at central observatories (5–15 nT) is comparable to the 2026 BOU-baseline range, not the W004-baseline range. A dome-defender will use this asymmetry to argue: 'PRED-TIER-3 lives in the W004-baseline ΔB regime where κ-arithmetic produces 10–13 µGal. 2009 lived in a BOU-baseline ΔB regime where κ-arithmetic predicts only 3–9 µGal — the two cases are not the same κ-prediction.' The vise still holds in principle — the dome must specify why the BOU-baseline regime does not trigger κ-coupling and the W004-baseline regime does — but our prose papers over the dual-baseline reality.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one clarifying sentence to detail_evidence (and the section 6.14 'What κ predicts during past eclipses' subsection) acknowledging the dual-baseline structure: 'The dome's 2026 predictions span two ΔB baselines — BOU-pred (~-5 to -13 nT, κ-implying 3–8 µGal) and W004-pred (~-10 to -26 nT, κ-implying 6–16 µGal). The 2009 measured ΔB at central observatories sits in the BOU-baseline range; either κ couples at both baselines (and 2009 refutes the BOU regime) or it does not (and PRED-TIER-3's W004-derived 10–13 µGal target has no published justification).' Closes the asymmetry without weakening the vise — both horns still lose.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence cites 'Crossley et al. 2013' with URL https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 — the journal-code+year prefix in that DOI (s00024-022-...) indicates the linked paper is a 2022 Pure and Applied Geophysics paper, not the canonical Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013 review in Reports on Progress in Physics (DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101). The text year (2013) does not match the URL's implied year (2022). The same mismatch propagates into section 6.14's 'What past SG data actually shows' subsection. Citation integrity has been a credibility lever we've used against the dome; a defender who notices this can argue 'you criticize the dome for sloppy citations while linking the wrong paper.' The substantive 0.1–1 µGal floor claim is correct on either citation, but the URL/year mismatch is a real bug.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) update the URL to the actual Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013 paper: https://iopscience.iop.org/article/10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 (DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101), or (b) update the cited year to 2022 and the author string to match the actual lead author of s00024-022-03000-4. Option (a) is cleaner because the 2013 RoPP review is the canonical SG noise-floor reference. Patch detail_evidence in WIN-013, WIN-014, and section 6.14 in one pass.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence states 'The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path,' but the eight Chinese SG sites are not on the path of totality uniformly. Wuhan, Lhasa, and other listed sites span latitudes and longitudes well outside the central path. A dome-defender will argue: 'The 5–15 nT central-observatory ΔB range applies to stations near totality. The Chinese SG sites were at coverage X% (likely 60–85% at most sites), so ΔB at those sites was scaled down by the coverage factor and the FSF — to perhaps 2–8 nT. Under κ, that predicts a Δg of 1–5 µGal at the SG sites, of which 1–2 µGal is at or below the 2009-era post-reduction floor at short timescales. The 2009 null is consistent with κ if you do the arithmetic at the actual SG site coverages.' We do not address this. The defense survives a first reading.
holes_found.recommendation: Add per-site coverage data for the eight 2009 SG stations. The dome's own FSF and coverage scaling can be inverted: even at 50% coverage, an ΔB of 3 nT through κ predicts 1.8 µGal, which is 2× above the 1 µGal floor at most SG sites. Cite either (a) the published 2009 campaign paper with per-site coverage maps, or (b) the dome's own coverage table for 2009 if one exists. If neither, shift the burden explicitly: the dome must specify which SG sites were on the path of sufficient coverage for κ-coupling to predict a measurable pulse.

holes_found.description: The 'Refuted by Data' verdict tier traditionally applies when independent empirical data refutes a dome claim. Here, the refutation arises by combining the dome's own published coupling κ (WIN-012) with the dome's own published null measurement (WIN-014). Whether this constitutes 'Refuted by Data' or 'Self-Contradicted-with-extra-steps' is a taxonomic edge. The HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-013-014-VERDICT-REFRAME-001 and EXP-301 explicitly endorse 'Refuted by Data' to foreground the empirical refutation, and the vise mechanism does add empirical bite that pure self-contradiction lacks. A careful taxonomist could still argue the underlying logical structure is self-contradiction (model + null ⇒ ⊥). The operator/analyst choice is defensible, and the new prose makes the empirical refutation explicit enough; flagging for advocate-mode awareness rather than verdict-revisit.
holes_found.recommendation: No verdict change required. Optional: if decider wants to preempt this critique, add one sentence to detail_verdict_text: 'This verdict is classified Refuted by Data rather than Self-Contradicted because the refutation rests on the dome's own SG measurement of 0 µGal across the network — an empirical observation — failing to match the gravity pulse the dome's own published coupling predicts. The self-contradiction is the structural pattern; the empirical failure is the data refutation.' Not blocking.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence states 'The 2009 campaign is the same instrument class as PRED-TIER-3 anticipates, run sixteen years earlier on the dome's own published mechanism: it returned a null.' Strict arithmetic: 2026 − 2009 = 17 years, not 16. Numerical imprecision — one-character edit. A sharp defender will notice and use it to seed a generic 'your numerical claims are loose' narrative.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'sixteen years earlier' with 'seventeen years earlier' in WIN-014 detail_evidence. One-line decider patch; not blocking.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence cites 'WIN-068's 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements' as supporting the 5–15 nT range for the 2009 ΔB. WIN-068's current verdict is 'Std Model Explains' — we reject the dome's interpretation while accepting the magnitudes from the underlying published ensemble. This is internally consistent (data values vs interpretation), but a hostile reader can argue we cherry-pick the magnitudes while rejecting the interpretation. Optics issue, not a substantive hole.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: replace the WIN-068 cross-reference with a direct citation to the underlying eclipse-magnetic ensemble paper (already linked via Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018), avoiding the cross-WIN dependency. One-line edit; not blocking.

holes_found.description: C3/C4/C5/C6 H8 still open as ISS-1829 (open, minor). 'Eight gravimeters were deployed across six sites' remains uncited in detail_evidence — the new prose retains the same uncited claim. No movement since c6.
holes_found.recommendation: No new recommendation; ISS-1829 holds the original c3 fix proposal. Decider should either patch the citation in a future cycle or downgrade if the source cannot be located.

holes_found.description: raw-text/monitor.py and raw-text/pull_data.py are not present in the current dome raw-text snapshot (verified by find on the curmudgeon clone). Prior cycles (c2 through c6) referenced grep results on monitor.py — those results cannot be replicated in this cycle from the local clone. This is not WIN-014-specific and does not undermine any WIN-014 tag value (monitoring:none is consistent with everything else), but it is a methodology gap worth flagging for the tinker/decider.
holes_found.recommendation: Tinker should investigate whether the raw-text pipeline (poller? separate fetch?) should be capturing monitor.py and pull_data.py. If yes, restore. If no, document the policy in monitor/prompts/reference/. Not blocking on WIN-014.

stronger_arguments.description: Make the BOU-baseline-vs-W004-baseline distinction explicit (H1's recommendation). The vise tightens: at BOU-baseline ΔB (~5–13 nT, matching 2009 observations), κ predicts 3–8 µGal — and the 2009 SG measured zero. At W004-baseline ΔB (~10–26 nT, matching dome's 2026 W004-pred), κ predicts 6–16 µGal — and that is what PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal target encodes. The 2009 null thus refutes κ-coupling in the lower-ΔB regime; the dome's only escape is to claim κ-coupling threshold-activates above some ΔB cutoff, which is a scope condition the dome has not published. Cleaner than the current implicit single-regime framing.

Address the SG-site-coverage objection (H3) by either pulling per-site coverage data for the 2009 SG stations or noting that the dome itself does not publish a per-station coverage table for 2009 backtest cases — the asymmetry between the dome's detailed 2026 station predictions and the absent 2009 per-site coverage is itself part of the unfalsifiability pattern.

kernel_of_truth.description: Two genuine insights survive the refutation. First, superconducting gravimeters are dramatically more sensitive than spring gravimeters at long periods, and the dome's framing of the 1999/2009 SG nulls as a meaningful test of an eclipse-gravity coupling is not absurd on its face — the instruments could in principle detect a coupling, so their nulls are evidentially meaningful (cite data/wins.json:014.detail_evidence 'continuously sampling at 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction precision'). Second, the 2009 East Asian eclipse was a well-instrumented event with multiple SG deployments, and the dome's decision to cite it as a test case is methodologically reasonable in principle (cite raw-text/02-wins.txt:92-93 — the dome cites Sun 2010 as confirming '0.0 µGal across network'). The contact-phase concentration argument — that eclipse-coupling effects, if real, would be strongest at C1/C4 when geometry changes most rapidly — has at least face-validity.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel is undermined by the dome's own forward-prediction structure. By publishing PRED-TIER-3 (cite data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3, claim text 'SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals'), the dome commits to a positive 10–13 µGal SG signature at C1/C4 in the same instrument class as the 2009 SG campaign. Through the dome's own κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (cite data/wins.json:012.claim 'Mag-gravity coupling 1.67'), 2009's documented 5–15 nT central-observatory ΔB implies a 3–9 µGal Δg at the SG sites — a measurement the SGs were positioned and precise enough to detect (cite the same continuous-sampling-at-0.1–1-µGal floor) and which they recorded as zero. The instrumental-detection-class kernel matters if the dome had not committed κ to a specific numerical conversion; with κ on the books and PRED-TIER-3 deriving a positive number from it, the kernel cancels itself.

deeper_analysis: The verdict change from Self-Contradicted to Refuted by Data is operator-commissioned (HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-013-014-VERDICT-REFRAME-001), analyst-endorsed (EXP-301), and well-grounded in the new prose. The refutation chain is clean: (1) dome publishes κ = 1.677 nT/µGal as a coupling constant; (2) 2009 documented ΔB at central observatories is 5–15 nT; (3) Δg = ΔB/κ ⇒ 3–9 µGal predicted; (4) SG noise floor 0.1–1 µGal ⇒ predicted pulse is 3×–90× above floor; (5) SG measured zero. The symmetric vise (any scope condition exempting 2009 also kills PRED-TIER-3) closes the published escape routes via the cluster section. The verdict change holds. Three substantive findings emerge that are worth a decider patch: H1 (the BOU-vs-W004 baseline ambiguity in PRED-TIER-3's derivation — a defender can use this), H5 (Crossley citation URL year-mismatch — citation-integrity bug), and H3 (SG-site-coverage objection — eight Chinese SG stations were not uniformly on the path of totality). H1 and H3 do not threaten the verdict but a defender can use them to argue our refutation is loose. H5 is a citation-integrity issue that should be cleaned up alongside the parallel mention in section 6.14. H6/ISS-1829 carries forward unchanged. H7 is a precautionary advocate-mode flag, not a verdict revisit. H8 is a tinker-level methodology note. The TLDRs are consistent with the verdict change; the c6 H19 affirmative-absence concern is mostly moot under the new κ-prediction-first framing. IMPORTANT ADDITION (this cycle): WIN-013.c5 (2026-05-10) Crossref-verified that the Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 cite URL (PII S0273117718300656) resolves to Park et al. on nanosatellite constellation deployment — NOT the eclipse-magnetic ensemble paper. This cite is one of the two pillars of WIN-014's 5–15 nT range; the EXP-301 integration imported the broken cite into WIN-014 without revalidation. H0 (critical) tracks this and recommends block-fix coordinated across WIN-013, WIN-014, and Section 6.14. The verdict change still holds because the refutation arithmetic (κ × ΔB) is sound at any plausible 5–15 nT range, but the cited source for that range needs replacement.

advocate_mode.best_defense: In addition to the BOU-vs-W004 baseline argument and the SG-site-coverage argument (both above), a dome-defender could attack the citation integrity directly: 'Your Refuted-by-Data verdict rests on a 5–15 nT 2009 ΔB range you source to Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 — but that URL resolves to a paper about nanosatellite constellation deployment, not eclipse magnetism. Either your reviewers have not checked their own citations, or this paper does not exist as cited. The empirical refutation chain has a load-bearing fabricated citation.' This is rhetorically devastating, especially given the dome author has demonstrated direct DM contact with our reviewers (per CLAUDE.md operator-contact addendum) and will likely actively check our citations. The κ-arithmetic is sound at any plausible ΔB, so the substantive case survives, but the citation integrity damage is real.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: URGENT — block-fix the Tomás cite (H0) BEFORE any further dome-side traffic to WIN-014. The cite has been broken since at least 2026-05-10 (WIN-013.c5) and has now propagated through EXP-301 into WIN-014 and Section 6.14. Decider should escalate to analyst for a replacement-paper search if the original Tomás reference cannot be located. After H0 is resolved, address H1 (BOU-vs-W004 baseline) and H3 (SG-site-coverage) in a follow-on pass. H5 (Crossley URL) is a separate citation-integrity issue and should be batched with H0 in one coordinated citation-cleanup patch.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-013 and WIN-014 both moved to Refuted by Data (EXP-301 P1 and P2 verified in this clone's data/wins.json) — the cross-WIN asymmetry flagged by c6 H15 is resolved. WIN-012 remains Self-Contradicted, which is correct (it IS the κ-definition that the new WIN-013/014 refutations rest on). WIN-058b at Not Demonstrated with cross-link added (EXP-301 P3 verified) — consistent with the κ-cluster section's storm-corollary treatment. WIN-010, WIN-011, WIN-068 retain pre-existing verdicts, appropriate for their narrower claims. The cluster is now internally consistent.

claims_checked: κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (from WIN-012); arithmetic Δg = ΔB/κ for ΔB ∈ [5, 15] nT ⇒ Δg ∈ [2.98, 8.94] µGal ≈ '3–9 µGal' — verified.

SG post-reduction noise floor 0.1–1 µGal — consistent with broadband Crossley-type figures; the WIN-014 0.1–1 µGal floor differs from WIN-013's 0.01–0.1 µGal Membach-specific floor (Van Camp 2001) — both defensible for their instruments.
Floor-multiple range: 3 µGal / 1 µGal = 3×; 9 µGal / 0.1 µGal = 90× — predicted pulse is 3×–90× above floor.

2026 W004-baseline ΔB range from raw-text/03-predictions.txt: E001 -21.7 nT to E005 -26.2 nT and surrounding stations; under κ that's 13.1–15.6 µGal, bracketing the 10–13 µGal PRED-TIER-3 target. BOU-baseline ΔB range: -5.0 to -12.8 nT; under κ that's 3.0–7.6 µGal — confirming H1's baseline-asymmetry observation.
Sixteen-vs-seventeen-years: 2026 − 2009 = 17 years, not 16. WIN-014 detail_evidence has the off-by-one (H2 confirmed).
quantitative_verification.computations: Pure arithmetic in node: κ = 1.677; ΔB inputs and Δg outputs computed by direct division. Citation-URL mismatch verified by parsing the DOI prefix s00024-022-... which identifies the linked paper as 2022 Pure and Applied Geophysics. WIN cross-references and verdict states verified by JSON.parse + .find on data/wins.json.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: (1) BOU-vs-W004 baseline ambiguity in PRED-TIER-3 derivation (H1) — substantive; (2) Crossley URL points to 2022 paper not 2013 (H5) — citation bug; (3) sixteen vs seventeen years (H2) — minor numerical slip.

summary_for_decider: WIN-014 verdict reframe Self-Contradicted → Refuted by Data is sound IN ITS ARITHMETIC, but the cited source for the 5–15 nT 2009 ΔB range (Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018, PII S0273117718300656) was Crossref-verified by WIN-013.c5 to resolve to a different paper (Park et al. on nanosatellite deployment). H0 (CRITICAL) tracks this — propagated unchanged from WIN-013.c5; EXP-301 imported the broken cite into WIN-014 without revalidation. Block-fix recommended BEFORE any further dome-side traffic. Other findings: H1 (major, BOU-vs-W004 baseline ambiguity), H5 (major, Crossley citation URL year-mismatch — also citation integrity), H3 (moderate, SG-site-coverage), H7 (moderate, taxonomy advocate-mode flag), H2/H4 (minor optics/numerics), H6 (carry-over ISS-1829), H8 (tinker methodology). Recommended action: minor_edit on prose, BUT H0 elevates to critical citation-fix-needed. Verdict change holds.
